commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Manual review and contributor action are still needed because the remaining blockers combine a public config/API product decision, contributor-supplied real behavior proof, and a narrow auth-boundary test repair.; Needs attention: The runtime direction appears reasonable, but the credential-boundary case still lacks effective regression coverage and final-head real behavior proof.; Review finding: [P2] Exerci... (sha=8e1cf6b8a60070072fc5851ff8b0419b6fdc45cd)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds `agents.defaults.compaction.fallbacks` for embedded compaction, updates schema/docs/help/model-ref and Codex doctor warning surfaces, and adds focused tests.

PR surface: Source +72, Tests +136, Docs +26, Generated 0. Total +234 across 21 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows the replacement-chain test asserts `authProfileId: undefined` without supplying a session `authProfileId`, so the prior wrong-profile regression would not be caught. For the feature itself, current main lacks the requested fallback config surface.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review and contributor action are still needed because the remaining blockers combine a public config/API product decision, contributor-supplied real behavior proof, and a narrow auth-boundary test repair.

target.title: feat(compaction): add embedded fallback models
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Adds an embedded OpenClaw compaction fallback chain so `agents.defaults.compaction.model` can have its own ordered backup models when the primary summarizer fails.
- Keeps the contract intentionally narrow: this PR covers embedded OpenClaw compaction, not Lossless/context-engine-owned summaries and not native Codex compaction.

Why does this matter now?

- #56781 asks for compaction fallbacks because long sessions currently depend on one compaction model staying available.
- #93125 showed the right direction but mixed the runtime contract with unrelated drift, so this is a clean replacement with the fallback semantics spelled out.

What is the intended outcome?

- `agents.defaults.compaction.fallbacks` is an explicit replacement chain for embedded compaction.
- If `compaction.model` is set and `compaction.fallbacks` is unset, behavior stays exact: one configured compaction model, no inherited chat fallback chain.
- If `compaction.fallbacks` is set, fallback attempts use those candidates directly and do not reapply the configured `compaction.model` inside each candidate attempt.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Lossless/context-engine `summaryModel` fallback support.
- Any Codex native compaction model override; native Codex compaction owns that path.
- Crabbox/Testbox proof; we do not have that workflow for this PR yet.

What does success look like?

- Embedded compaction can move from a failed configured compaction model to the configured compaction fallback candidate.
- Docs, config schema/help/labels, model-ref collection, doctor warnings/repairs, and tests describe the same replacement-chain contract.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The fallback candidate order in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.ts`.
- The Codex boundary in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.ts` and doctor handling.
- Whether the explicit replacement-chain contract is the right product shape for the embedded slice.

## Linked context

Which issue is this related to?

Refs #56781

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #93125

Remaining work from #56781 stays open: Lossless/LCM and context-engine-owned `summaryModel` fallback support are deliberately not implemented here and should be handled in a follow-up once maintainers accept the embedded compaction contract.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- #93125 was closed with maintainer feedback asking for a clean design PR that defines replacement semantics, covers compaction-owner boundaries, asserts the actual provider/model attempted, and keeps LCM separate or explicitly scoped out.

AI assistance disclosure:

- This PR was implemented with AI assistance. I reviewed the affected compaction, config, Codex-boundary, doctor, docs, and test paths; I understand the patch and the proof recorded below.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: embedded compaction fallback routing from a configured compaction model to `agents.defaults.compaction.fallbacks`.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout at `b4c0aae8747bea1e3e99c1f361bd95d69fd7e762` for the real provider-routing proof; final head `8e1cf6b8a60070072fc5851ff8b0419b6fdc45cd` adds the ClawSweeper auth-boundary repair with focused regression coverage, disposable `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, local Ollama provider with `qwen3:8b`; no live cloud API keys were present.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: direct embedded compaction call through `compactEmbeddedAgentSessionDirect` with `compaction.model=ollama/qwen3:8b-does-not-exist` and `compaction.fallbacks=["ollama/qwen3:8b"]`, using isolated config/state under `/tmp`.
- Evidence after fix: redacted runtime log excerpt:

```text
[compaction-diag] ... attempt=start provider=ollama/qwen3:8b-does-not-exist
[compaction-diag] ... attempt=end provider=ollama/qwen3:8b-does-not-exist outcome=failed reason=Summarization_failed:_404_error_:_model_qwen3:8b-does-not-exist_not_found
[model-fallback] requested=ollama/qwen3:8b-does-not-exist candidate=ollama/qwen3:8b-does-not-exist reason=model_not_found next=ollama/qwen3:8b detail=Summarization failed: 404 {"error":"model 'qwen3:8b-does-not-exist' not found"}
[compaction-diag] ... attempt=start provider=ollama/qwen3:8b
[compaction-diag] ... provider=ollama/qwen3:8b outcome=failed reason=timeout durationMs=127940
[model-fallback] candidate=ollama/qwen3:8b reason=timeout next=none detail=Compaction timed out
{"ok":false,"compacted":false,"reason":"All models failed (2): ollama/qwen3:8b-does-not-exist: ... model_not_found | ollama/qwen3:8b: Compaction timed out (timeout)"}
```

- Observed result after fix: the fallback candidate was attempted by the real provider path, and the configured `compaction.model` was not reapplied inside the fallback attempt. The fallback attempt timed out after about 128s on local Ollama, so this proves fallback routing and candidate selection, not a successful summary.
- What was not tested: successful fallback summary output, Lossless/context-engine summary fallback behavior, native Codex compaction overrides, and Crabbox/Testbox.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: no live OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini keys were available. `pnpm openclaw`/gateway-backed command proof was blocked by an unrelated unstaged local gateway handoff import outside this PR. `agent --local --message "/compact"` treated `/compact` as a normal prompt rather than dispatching the compaction control path, so I used the embedded compaction entrypoint directly.
- Before evidence: #93125 ClawSweeper review found that the earlier branch mixed fallback semantics and included broad unrelated drift.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

Before the rebase:

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.test.ts src/config/config.compaction-settings.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts packages/model-catalog-core/src/configured-model-refs.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm config:docs:check`
- `corepack pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `git diff --cached --check`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core` was attempted after the fix; it reports only the unrelated unstaged `src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.ts` missing import, not this PR's staged diff.

After rebasing onto `main`:

- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `corepack pnpm config:docs:check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.test.ts src/config/config.compaction-settings.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts packages/model-catalog-core/src/configured-model-refs.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts`

After the ClawSweeper auth-boundary repair:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.test.ts src/config/config.compaction-settings.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts packages/model-catalog-core/src/configured-model-refs.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `git diff --check`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Embedded compaction tests for exact explicit-model behavior, configured fallback replacement, empty strict fallback mode, and fallback attempts that do not reapply `compaction.model`.
- Config schema/help tests for the new `agents.defaults.compaction.fallbacks` field.
- Configured-model ref collection coverage for `agents.defaults.compaction.fallbacks[*]`.
- Codex app-server and doctor coverage so native Codex compaction warns/removes ignored compaction `model/provider/fallbacks` overrides.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- There was no config surface for embedded compaction fallback candidates.
- The closed draft PR's documented fallback chain did not match the runtime replacement behavior.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added for the changed runtime, config, model-ref, Codex warning, and doctor surfaces.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. This adds `agents.defaults.compaction.fallbacks`.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, in the provider-routing sense: compaction may try another configured provider/model after a fallback-eligible failure.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Provider/auth routing during session compaction, especially cross-provider fallbacks.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The fallback list is opt-in and only applies to embedded compaction.
- Unset `compaction.fallbacks` preserves the existing exact `compaction.model` behavior.
- Codex native compaction and context-engine-owned compaction are documented as separate owner paths.
- Model-ref collection and doctor warnings were updated so the new config surface is visible to existing hygiene/migration flows.

Behavior tradeoffs:

- This chooses an explicit replacement chain instead of inheriting the chat fallback chain, so operators opt into compaction-specific backup models and do not get surprising compaction provider drift by default.

Scope boundaries:

- This PR leaves #56781 open. The Lossless/LCM and context-engine-owned `summaryModel` fallback remainder stays tracked there for a follow-up.
- Native Codex compaction remains owned by the Codex harness path and only gets warnings/doctor cleanup for ignored OpenClaw-side overrides.


Review metric:

- Added config/default surfaces: 1, `agents.defaults.compaction.fallbacks`. This matters because it is a public provider-routing knob; reviewers should confirm the replacement-chain contract before merge.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Draft PR stays open for maintainer review; do not mark ready until you explicitly approve it. ClawSweeper review has been run and its actionable PR-body rank-up moves have been addressed here.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Maintainer decision on whether embedded replacement semantics are the desired first slice.
- Follow-up tracking for the Lossless/LCM and context-engine-owned summary fallback remainder from #56781.
- Optional stronger real proof with a successful fallback summary if a live provider or faster local model is available.

Changelog handling:

- No contributor `CHANGELOG.md` edit. Release-note context is in this PR body for maintainers to use if this lands.

CODEOWNERS and owner review:

- The touched surfaces span agents, config, docs, commands, model catalog, and the bundled Codex plugin boundary; maintainer owner review is expected before this draft is marked ready.


Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Addressed #93125 feedback by removing unrelated drift, making `compaction.fallbacks` replacement semantics explicit, avoiding configured-model reapplication on fallback attempts, documenting Codex/context-engine ownership, and adding targeted tests.
- Addressed the #93331 ClawSweeper requests by adding the redacted proof log excerpt above, stating how the Lossless/LCM fallback remainder stays tracked, and repairing the cross-provider compaction auth-profile boundary.
